label: nested-endpoint
headline_statement: Confirmed nested endpoint. Under Assumptions~\ref{ass:iid-overlap}--\ref{ass:tail}, for every fixed policy \(\pi\), \(\sup_{Q\in\mathcal M_\Gamma(P)}\operatorname{CVaR}_{\tau}\{Y_Q(\pi)\}\) equals the computable nested functional \[\underline W_{\tau,\Gamma}(\pi;P)=\inf_{\eta\in[y_L,y_U]}\left\{\eta+\tau^{-1}\E_P\left[\sum_{a\in\mathcal A}\pi(a\mid X)\mathfrak T_{\Gamma}^{+}\{(Y-\eta)_+\mid a,X\}\right]\right\}.\] Equivalently, if \(e_a(x)=P(A=a\mid X=x)\), \(\ell_a(x)=e_a(x)+(1-e_a(x))/\Gamma\), and \(u_a(x)=e_a(x)+\Gamma(1-e_a(x))\), then for each nonnegative generated cell loss \(Z=(Y-\eta)_+\), \[\mathfrak T_{\Gamma}^{+}(Z\mid a,x)=\sup_{\ell_a(x)\le r\le u_a(x),\, \E_P[r(Y)\mid A=a,X=x]=1}\E_P[r(Y)Z\mid A=a,X=x],\] and the supremum is attained by the monotone least-favorable tilt that assigns weight \(u_a(x)\) to the largest values of \(Z\), weight \(\ell_a(x)\) to the smallest values of \(Z\), and only splits mass at the single threshold needed to make the conditional mean of \(r\) equal one. Thus \(\underline W_{\tau,\Gamma}(\pi;P)\) is the sharp upper endpoint of \(\Theta_I(P)\): every \(c\ge \underline W_{\tau,\Gamma}(\pi;P)\) is a valid upper bound for \(\operatorname{CVaR}_{\tau}\{Y_Q(\pi)\}\) over \(Q\in\mathcal M_\Gamma(P)\), and every \(c<\underline W_{\tau,\Gamma}(\pi;P)\) is violated by some compatible law.
